narrative:
On June 29, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 44.4%, with 1,015 of the 2,284 scheduled cases heard by 41 judges. This activity marks a slight decline from the 45.9% rate observed on June 25, but a minor increase over the 43.4% recorded on June 24.

Judicial efficiency highlights show that Justice H.P. Sandesh in Court Hall 8 resolved a heavy docket, hearing 80 of 109 cases (73.4%). Justice V. Srishananda in Court Hall 21 maintained high disposal with 62 of 82 cases heard (75.6%). In contrast, Court Hall 17 under Justice Ravi V. Hosmani heard none of its 76 scheduled cases, while Justice B.M. Shyam Prasad in Court Hall 6 heard just 9.8% of a major 112-case list, despite clearing 93.3% of a smaller 15-case list.

Stage-wise conversion shows that preliminary and procedural matters moved fastest. Cases listed for Orders had a 54.6% hearing rate (363 of 665), up from 48.4% on June 25. Admissions progressed at 52.5% (202 of 385). Conversely, Final Hearings lagged, converting only 23.8% of 626 scheduled matters, down from 31.2% on June 25.

By case type, Writ Petitions (WP) dominated the docket. The court heard 41.6% of its 1,024 scheduled WPs (426 cases), a drop in efficiency from the 46.3% of 985 WPs heard on June 25. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) saw 140 of 305 cases heard (45.9%), while Criminal Revision Petitions (CRL.RP) achieved the highest clearance, converting 82.2% of its 73 scheduled cases.
